name: McDonald's Corporation

profile.description: McDonald's is the world's largest restaurant company by revenue, operating and franchising 45,356 restaurants across 100+ countries. 95% franchised model generates $26.9B in 2025 revenue with systemwide sales of $139.4B.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): 2025 revenue of $26.9B (+4%), systemwide sales of $139.4B (+7%), Q4 same-store sales +5.7% with U.S. +6.8% demonstrating strong momentum [1]. Opened 2,276 restaurants in 2025. AI Deployment (2/2): AI-powered drive-through ordering deployed across locations, dynamic pricing/personalization through the McDonald's app [1]. Market Share (2/2): World's largest restaurant company by systemwide sales, dominant global QSR brand. Pipeline (0/2): Mid-to-high 40% operating margin guidance and 2.5% unit growth. R&D Intensity (1/1): Above-average tech investment for restaurants [1].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Clear mission to serve communities through affordable food. Community Impact (2/2): 150,000 corporate employees plus millions of franchise jobs; Ronald McDonald House and significant charitable programs [1]. Retention (1/2): Large franchise-based workforce with mixed retention. Insider (1/2): Management equity alignment [1].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: McDonald's demonstrates exceptional financial discipline. Forward P/E of 21.22 is slightly above target, but profit margin of 31.85% far exceeds the 20% target [1]. Revenue growth of 9.7% in Q4 and 4% full-year is solid. Dividend yield of 2.44% with 60% payout is sustainable with 48 consecutive years of growth. ROE data unavailable due to negative equity from buybacks. Systemwide sales of $139.4B provides massive economic scale [1].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): Restaurant operations involve physical food preparation, service, and real estate that AI cannot substitute [1]. The franchise model adds regulatory and relationship moats. AI enhances rather than threatens — drive-through AI, personalization, and operational optimization improve margins. AI Opportunity (1/1): AI drive-through ordering, dynamic pricing, and app personalization generate measurable efficiency gains [1].

infinite_game.summary: McDonald's is the archetype of infinite-game thinking in restaurants — 70+ years of continuous adaptation from drive-ins to McCafe to digital/AI, always evolving the model while maintaining the core Just Cause of serving affordable food globally.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause IS the competitive advantage (3 pts → 10/10): Making delicious feel-good moments easy for everyone — validated by 70+ years, 45,356 restaurants, 100+ countries, and multi-generational brand loyalty [1].

trusting_teams.analysis: Adequate (1 pt → 6/10): Massive franchise system with mixed labor relations but stable leadership [1].

worthy_rivals.analysis: Finite (0 pts → 3/10): Competitive language focuses on beating Burger King, Wendy's rather than learning [1].

existential_flexibility.analysis: Some (1 pt → 6/10): Transformed from burger restaurant to global platform — breakfast, McCafe, digital ordering, delivery [1].

courage_to_lead.analysis: Some (1 pt → 6/10): Major digital transformation investment and AI drive-through rollout [1].

investment_thesis: McDonald's is a global franchise machine delivering $26.9B revenue (+4%), $139.4B systemwide sales (+7%), and Q4 same-store sales of +5.7% with U.S. +6.8% [1]. 31.85% profit margin, 2.44% dividend yield with 48 consecutive years of growth, and 95% franchise model create a compounding wealth machine. AI drive-through ordering and digital personalization provide the next margin expansion lever. 2,276 new restaurants in 2025 with mid-to-high 40% operating margin guidance [1].
thesis_continued: The bear case includes forward P/E of 21.22 offering modest upside, and management expects weaker Q1 2026 same-store growth [1]. Consumer spending sensitivity in a potential recession could pressure traffic. Wage inflation and input costs pressure franchise economics. Negative book equity from buybacks creates leveraged downside. International geopolitical risks across 100+ countries [1].
key_catalysts: AI drive-through and digital ordering expansion improving margins [1]
Same-store sales momentum with U.S. +6.8% in Q4 [1]
key_risks: Consumer spending weakness pressuring same-store sales [1]
Wage and input cost inflation squeezing franchise economics [1]
International geopolitical and currency risks across 100+ countries [1]
sources: McDonald's Q4/FY2025 Results — $26.9B revenue, $139.4B systemwide sales, 5.7% SSS, 2,276 openings, February 2026
McDonald's 2026 Outlook — Mid-to-high 40% operating margin, 2.5% unit expansion, $3.7-3.9B capex
Yahoo Finance / yfinance data for MCD (McDonald's), retrieved April 2026
